_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in applying cybersecurity methods, such as firewalls, demilitarized zones, and encryption.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI security assessments, including threat modeling for AI systems, attack surface analysis, and control gap identification.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-S-008 (SP, cosine: 0.587), AI-S-010 (AI, cosine: 0.520).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.4/10 (raw 0.438). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. DCWF-only terms [applying, cybersecurity, demilitarized, encryption, firewalls, methods] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, analysis, assessments, attack, conducting, control] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.879 (P=0.879, R=0.879). Balanced P/R — the 12-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (78.2%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in applying cybersecurity methods, such as firewalls, demilitarized zones," and "Skill in conducting AI security assessments, including threat modeling for AI sy". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.605 > STS 0.438 (gap +0.168). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.1%. Shared tokens: [skill]. DCWF-only: [applying, cybersecurity, demilitarized, encryption, firewalls, methods]. WIDAI-only: [ai, analysis, assessments, attack, conducting, control].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 78.2% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
STS 0.438 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.168 with low STS 0.438. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
